summary: Goodwill bounty #49: published an original public field walkthrough for runx (runx.ai) — a durable GitHub-hosted guide covering the two-file skill shape (SKILL.md + X.yaml), the local `runx harness` proof gate including exact `expect.status` contract values learned from live rejection, and the local-vs-remote publish pitfall (missing --registry flag). It links both runx.ai and github.com/runxhq/runx, and is reachable by a stranger at a stable raw URL. Authentic support action; not star-only, not a screenshot, not link spam.

observations.result: Walkthrough hosted at raw.githubusercontent.com — returns HTTP 200 to anonymous strangers, no auth or WAF gate. Immutable per commit.

Fresh field note written from live runx experience — covers two-file shape, harness expect.status values (sealed|failure|needs_agent|policy_denied|escalated — NOT 'failed'), and the --registry publish trap.
Walkthrough explicitly links runx.ai and github.com/runxhq/runx — satisfies the acceptance criterion that the public artifact must reference runx.
GitHub raw content is a public, durable host for original OSS documentation. Project mentions and walkthroughs are explicitly permitted there. Not a star screenshot, not spam, no reciprocal-star ask.
